observations.correspondence_rationale: The passage describes two contrasting responses to loss: one who discards all (burns and scatters ashes) and one who clings tightly to remnants, paralleling the parable of the Prodigal Son where the son leaves and the father awaits restoration, highlighting themes of loss, grief, and reconciliation.
observations.summary: The text's depiction of grief responses parallels the Prodigal Son's narrative of loss and restoration in Luke 15, illustrating the tension between letting go and holding on.

evidence.quote: Some people throw everything away when they lose the thing they love most—burn it all, scatter the ashes, start fresh with empty hands. Others keep every thread, every cracked vessel, every scrap of parchment with nothing written on it, and pull it tighter and tighter until there is no room left to breathe.

accepted_observations.correspondence_rationale: The passage describes two contrasting responses to loss: one who discards all (burns and scatters ashes) and one who clings tightly to remnants, paralleling the parable of the Prodigal Son where the son leaves and the father awaits restoration, highlighting themes of loss, grief, and reconciliation.
accepted_observations.summary: The text's depiction of grief responses parallels the Prodigal Son's narrative of loss and restoration in Luke 15, illustrating the tension between letting go and holding on.
